metaswarm_flag_handling.summary: Flag parsed correctly; sets METASWARM=true, creates commander dirs, switches agent to stampede-commander, caps count at 5.
bugs: Preflight hardcodes stampede-agent even in metaswarm mode (should use stampede-commander)
Cap message says 1600 total workers but 5x250=1250

commander_pane_creation.summary: Pane creation is identical mechanism (tmux split-window); difference is in script content, agent name, env vars, and pane titles.
details: Commander panes titled: Commander #N · model · full SS-250
Commander-specific env vars: STAMPEDE_COMMANDER_ID, STAMPEDE_PROGRESS_DIR, collab vars
Failure handling writes to swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl

model_rotation.summary: Deterministic round-robin via modulo. Same logic for both workers and commanders. --models overrides --model.
bugs: Bash IFS split vs Python split disagree on trailing commas (fleet.json may mismatch actual model)
No whitespace trimming on model tokens
Empty model entries not validated (can pass --model empty string to CLI)
Preflight only probes first model in rotation

pid_tracking.summary: PIDs captured after 5s sleep via process tree walk. Commander PIDs use commander-NNN.pid naming. Sub-agent PIDs invisible to launcher.
bugs: 5-second hardcoded sleep is a race condition (Landmine #16)
head -n1 in find_leaf_pid may capture tee instead of copilot
No trap for unexpected launcher crash - orphans tmux session
PID files not written atomically (no .tmp+mv pattern)
Commander sub-agent PIDs completely untracked by launcher

tmux_session_management.summary: Single session stampede-{RUN_ID} with one window, tiled panes. Existing session killed before launch. Monitor in pane 0.
details: Pre-existing session detected and killed before new launch
Older tmux compatibility handled via 2>/dev/null || true on cosmetic features
No automatic cleanup on Ctrl+C (only --teardown explicit path)
Layout identical for normal/metaswarm; difference is semantic role/title only

overall_assessment.verdict: Launcher synchronization and metaswarm support is structurally sound. Core paths work correctly. Several edge-case bugs exist (preflight validation mismatch, PID race, model parsing inconsistencies) but none are blockers for normal operation.
